posting.title: Audio Software Engineer Needed for Worship Playback Platform
posting.clientContext: Worship music team seeking audio engineer to audit, fix, and stabilize existing desktop music playback application
posting.scope: Initial engagement: review codebase, identify issues, fix high-priority bugs, recommend improvements

four_application_questions: Have you worked on audio software, music applications, playback systems, DAWs, audio plugins, or live performance software?
What technologies and frameworks were used?
What was your role on the project?
Please provide examples or links if available

michael_credentials_to_emphasize.education: Bachelor's degree in Music from St. Olaf College — formally trained musician
michael_credentials_to_emphasize.daw_experience: Using DAWs since 5th grade — decades of hands-on audio production and musical knowledge
michael_credentials_to_emphasize.live_audio_professional: Working wedding DJ with obsessive attention to live audio quality and perfect sound
michael_credentials_to_emphasize.real_time_audio_systems: Built production-ready Python MIDI/audio bridge for live DJ signal processing: bridges out-of-date DJ controller MIDI/output to newer laptop and DJ software, actively used during live gigs, proves real-time audio system and desktop integration skills
michael_credentials_to_emphasize.positioning: Musician-engineer hybrid: combines formal music training, deep DAW expertise, live performance experience, and production-quality software engineering — exactly what the client needs

demo_brief.concept: Browser-based worship multitrack playback engine (Web Audio API) loading real free/CC-licensed worship stems, demonstrating mastery of every pain point the client listed
demo_brief.hero_feature: Sample-accurate multitrack transport + mixer: rock-solid play/pause/stop/seek with sample-accurate Web Audio scheduling, per-stem channel routing (drums/bass/keys/vocals/click/guide), per-channel volume/mute/solo/pan/output, master bus
supporting_features: Section navigation: arrangement markers (Intro/Verse/Chorus/Bridge/Tag), click-to-jump, loop a section
Click track + count-in: generated metronome locked to tempo map, bar-1 accent, configurable count-in bars before playback
demo_brief.tertiary_if_budget_allows: Guide cues: spoken or visual cues ('Big finish', 'To the chorus') fired at scheduled beats
demo_brief.design: Pro stage/console aesthetic (dark near-black background, high-contrast meters, amber/teal LED accent glow, monospace numerics). Reference Ableton Live, Loop Community Prime, MultiTracks Playback. NOT michaelwegter.com portfolio style.
demo_brief.audio_sources: Bundle CC-licensed free worship/instrumental stems or generate via synth to avoid licensing risk
demo_brief.build_form: Vite app (complexity justified by audio scheduling + mixer UI), source at upwork-runs/audio-software-engineer-needed-for/demo-src/, base='/demos/audio-software-engineer-needed-for/', copy dist/* to public demos folder. Frontend-only (no backend by default).
demo_brief.richness_definition: 10/10 = genuinely full-featured, production-quality app with several working features, polished, ready to impress

quality_gates.gate_1: Every explicit requirement (9 pain points + 4 application questions) addressed in cover letter AND represented in demo or deck
quality_gates.gate_2: Demo builds and loads with no console errors on first paint; sample-accurate Web Audio scheduling (lookahead scheduler, not setTimeout); sync verified visually
quality_gates.gate_3: Demo URL /demos/audio-software-engineer-needed-for/ resolves locally and after deploy /work-samples/audio-software-engineer-needed-for returns 200
quality_gates.gate_4: Demo link appears in cover letter AND deck
quality_gates.gate_5: Cohesive, bespoke design fit for pro worship-playback aesthetic (not portfolio look); no em/en dashes in any copy
quality_gates.gate_6: Traceability matrix mapping all 9 pain points to concrete demo features or deck points

constraints.build_caps: Demo touches at most 12 files; build timeout 3 minutes; stays within feature richness 10 without exceeding scope
constraints.no_dashes: Never use em-dashes or en-dashes in cover letter, deck, one-pager, or any prose. Use commas, periods, or rewrite.

deliverables.demo: Live, working worship playback engine at /work-samples/audio-software-engineer-needed-for
deliverables.cover_letter: Address client's 4 application questions explicitly via Michael's credentials and live demo link
deliverables.one_pager: HTML, cohesive design, demo link, feature overview
deliverables.deck: PowerPoint or PDF, Michael's background + demo link + feature traceability to the 9 pain points

proposal_narrative: Michael's proposal is NOT 'I can code' but 'I am a musician-engineer with a decade+ of DAW expertise, live audio professional chops, real-time system experience, and production-software skills—this worship-playback audit is exactly my sweet spot.' The live demo proves he understands the domain deeply: every feature he built addresses one of their 9 pain points, proving he can reason about multitrack playback architecture end to end. The MIDI bridge example shows he ships production audio systems.
